_note: CURATED overrides on top of the auto-extracted lore.json. Power-system ladders here are hand-authored and VERIFIED against the indexed chapters (every tier name confirmed to appear in the text). The loader prefers these over the extracted power_system; glossary stays from extraction. This is the per-novel curation layer of the shared template.

power_system.name: Gu Master Cultivation
power_system.summary: Cultivators ('Gu Masters') raise mystical Gu worms in a personal aperture fueled by primeval essence. Strength is measured in Ranks 1-9; each rank has stages initial/middle/upper/peak. Mastery of a 'path' (Dao) is counted in dao marks.

ladder.note: Mortal cultivators. Rank 5 is the mortal ceiling.
Ascended via refining an Immortal Gu + surviving heavenly tribulation; aperture becomes a Blessed Land. Uses immortal essence (does not regenerate).
Only a handful in all history; reshape the world and leave 'true inheritances'.

mechanics: Gu are refined, hunted, traded, combined into 'killer moves'.
Mortal essence regenerates; immortal essence does NOT — permanent scarcity.
Heaven's Will: a sentient fate that shields destined figures and obstructs heretics; 'qi fortune' (luck) can be plundered.

power_system.name: Profound Cultivation (Profound Realms → Divine Way)
power_system.summary: Practitioners refine Profound Energy and ascend the nine Profound Realms, each with 10 internal levels, then break into the Divine Way. Profound strength, profound arts, and profound veins/constellations determine power.

ladder.note: Apex of the mortal Profound Realms.

mechanics: Each Profound Realm has 10 levels (and within those, early/middle/peak).
Profound energy carries attributes (fire, ice, etc.); profound veins set talent.

power_system.name: Cultivation (Qi Condensation → Immortal Ascension)
power_system.summary: Cultivators refine qi through the classic realm ladder, each realm gated by a bottleneck/tribulation. Beyond Immortal Ascension lie higher, less-common realms.

ladder.note: Becoming an Ascendant; higher realms (e.g. Dao Realm) exist beyond.

mechanics: Battle prowess can exceed realm via techniques, treasures, and bloodline.

power_system.name: Cultivation (Qi Condensation → Deva)
power_system.summary: Same broad cultivation framework as the author's other works: refine qi up the realm ladder toward the Deva Realm. Pill/spirit-medicine refinement is a major theme.

ladder.note: Apex realm reached in the story.

mechanics: Medicinal pills and spirit medicines accelerate cultivation; medicinal toxins accumulate.
Merit points buy resources within sects.

power_system.name: Awakened Ascension (non-numeric)
power_system.summary: Not a numeric cultivation ladder. Ordinary people are Sleepers; those who survive the Nightmare Spell become Awakened, gaining an Aspect and Aspect Abilities. Power grows by surviving deeper Nightmares and collecting Memories; each Awakened has a hidden True Name. Strength is described by ascension tiers.

ladder.note: Newly entering the Dream Realm / on the cusp of awakening.

mechanics: Awakened gain an Aspect (their core power) and Aspect Abilities.
Power grows via surviving Nightmares and absorbing Memories.
Every Awakened guards a secret True Name; soul/shadow powers are central.

power_system.name: Magus Ranks
power_system.summary: Practitioners are Magi who cultivate spiritual force and knowledge, ascending Magus grades. Bloodlines are graded and matter greatly. Higher 'transcendent' tiers exist beyond the standard Magus ranks.

ladder.note: Higher transcendent tier (order of top tiers per chapters).

mechanics: Spiritual force, meditation, and magic-array/rune work drive power.
Bloodlines are graded (up to and beyond Grade A) and confer strong traits.
power_system._confidence: medium — standard Magus ranks verified; exact order of the top transcendent tiers should be confirmed against later chapters.

power_system.name: Cultivation (early-to-mid verified)
power_system.summary: Classic qi-refinement cultivation ladder. The early-to-mid realms below are verified; this novel's higher realms vary by translation and should be confirmed against the chapters.

ladder.note: Higher realms (e.g. Illusory Yin/Yang and beyond) exist — defer to chapters for exact order.

mechanics: Breakthroughs are perilous and resource-hungry; the protagonist's path is famously grim and self-reliant.
power_system._confidence: medium — early/mid realms verified; later realms intentionally left to retrieval.
